person.userCertifiedLifeSummary:
geni.com
Graaf Albert II van Namen
Finnish: Albert II de Namur, comte de Namur, Swedish: Albert II de Namur, comte de Namur, French: Comte Albert de Namur
Also Known As:	"Adalbert de Namur"
Birthdate:	circa 997
Birthplace:	Namur, Walloon Region, Belgium
Death:	November 15, 1037 (35-44)
Namur, Walloon Region, Belgium
Immediate Family:	
Son of Albert I, count of Namur and Ermengarde
Husband of Regilinde of Lorraine

Father of
 Heinrich II von Durbuy la Roche; 
Hedwige of Lorraine; 
Albert III de Namur, Count of Namur and 
Ermengard IV van Namen

Brother of Ermengarde III of Namur, Countess of Vermandois; Henri de Namur, comte de Durbuy; Goda de Namur; Beatrix de Lorraine and Luitgard Emma van Namen


Occupation:	Conde de Namur, Comte, de Namur, Graaf van Namen, COUNT OF NAMUR, 1000 - 1064, Comte de Namur, graaf van Namen

Albert II of Namur was Count of Namur from the death of his elder brother Robert in 1016 to Albert's death in 1067. 
They were the sons of Albert I, and Ermengarde daughter of Charles, Duke of Lower Lorraine.

Biography
In 1037, Albert participated in the war against Odo II, Count of Blois who sought to create a kingdom between France and Germany. In 1046, he took advantage of the Emperor Henry III in his fight against Godfrey III, Duke of Lower Lorraine, and Baldwin V, Count of Flanders.

In 1047 he founded the Collegiate church of St. Alban at Namur.

«b»Marriages and issue«/b»
Between 1010 and 1015 he married Regelinde (d.1067) daughter of Gothelo I, Count of Verdun and Duke of Lorraine and had the following issue:

1.) Albert III (1027-1102)

2.) Henry I, Count of Durbuy (d.1097 in Palestine)

3.) Hedwige of Namur, married Gérard, Duke of Lorraine
__________________________________________________

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Albert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old French name, Albert, of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from adal ‘noble’ + berht ‘bright, famous’. This was adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to England, displacing the Old English form Æþelbeorht. The name is popular in a variety of forms in Western Europe, and has been traditional in a number of European princely families. It was out of favour in England for centuries, however, and the revival of its popularity in the 19th century was largely in honour of Queen Victoria's consort, Prince Albert of Saxe-Coburg-Gotha .

nameMeanings.name: II
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of   Violet  ,   Vivien  , or   Vivian .

variants.name: Vivien
meanings: Earlier generally taken as a variant of the boy's name Vivian , but now also rarely used for boys. This spelling was quite common in Old French. Its use as a girl's name in the English-speaking world was influenced by Tennyson's Merlin and Vivien ( 1859 ). This name, from Arthurian legend, may represent an altered form of a Celtic name (perhaps akin to the Irish Gaelic name Béibhinn ‘white lady’, pronounced ‘bee-veen’). The actress Vivien Leigh ( 1913–67 ) was christened Vivian.

variants.name: Violet
meanings: From the name of the flower (Old French violette, Late Latin violetta, a diminutive of viola). This was one of the earliest flower names to become popular in Britain, being used as early as 1700 and becoming well established in the 19th century, although it is now somewhat out of favour.

variants.name: Vivian
meanings: Originally a boy's name, from an Old French form of the Latin name Vivianus (probably a derivative of vivus ‘alive’), but now more frequent as a girl's name. The name was borne by a 5th-century bishop of Saintes in western France, remembered for protecting his people during the invasion of the Visigoths.

en.lifeSummary: When Albert II de Namur was born in 0997, in Namur, Belgium, his father, Albert Ier comte de Namur, was 24 and his mother, Ermengard von Lothringen, was 26. He married Regelindis de Lorraine in 1025. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 1 daughter. He died about 1063, in Namur, Holy Roman Empire, at the age of 67.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Albert II de Namur est né en 0997, à Namur, Belgique, son père, Albert Ier comte de Namur, avait 24 ans et sa mère, Ermengard von Lothringen, avait 26 ans. Il a épousé Regelindis de Lorraine en 1025. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 1 fille. Il est décédé environ 1063, à Namur, Saint-Empire romain, à l’âge de 67 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Albert II de Namur 0997 in Namen, Belgien geboren wurde, war sein Vater Albert Ier comte de Namur 24 und seine Mutter Ermengard von Lothringen 26. Er hat 1025 Regelindis de Lorraine geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Er starb um 1063 im Alter von 67 Jahren in Namen, Heiliges Römisches Reich.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Albert II de Namur è nato il nel 0997 nel Namur, Belgio, il suo padre, Albert Ier comte de Namur, aveva 24 anni e il suo madre, Ermengard von Lothringen, aveva 26 anni. Lui sposato Regelindis de Lorraine nel 1025. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 1 figlia. Morì circa 1063, nel Namur, Heiliges Römisches Reich, all'età di 67 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Albert II de Namurが0997年にベルギーナムルで生まれた時，父Albert Ier comte de Namurは24歳，母Ermengard von Lothringenは26歳であった。1025年にRegelindis de Lorraineと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼は約1063年にヘイリジェズ ルoミシェス レイチナムルで67歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Albert II de Namur(이)가 0997년 벨기에 남어에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Albert Ier comte de Namur(은)는 24세였고, 그의 어머니 Ermengard von Lothringen(은)는 26세였다. 그는 1025년 Regelindis de Lorraine(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 대략 1063년 헤이리그스 o미슈스 라이치 남어에서 67세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Albert II de Namur nasceu em 0997, em Namur, Bélgica, seu pai, Albert Ier comte de Namur, tinha 24 anos e sua mãe, Ermengard von Lothringen, tinha 26 anos. Ele casou-se com Regelindis de Lorraine em 1025. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 1 filha. Ele faleceu aproximadamente 1063, em Namur, Heiliges Römisches Reich, com 67 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Albert II de Namur родился в 0997 г. в местности намур, Бельгия, его отцу по имени Albert Ier comte de Namur было 24 года, а матери, которую звали Ermengard von Lothringen, 26. Он женился на женщине по имени Regelindis de Lorraine в 1025 г. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 1 дочь. Он умер около 1063 г. в местности намур, хилигесь рoмищесь раич в возрасте 67 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Albert II de Namur nació en 0997, en Namur, Bélgica, su papá, Albert Ier comte de Namur, tenía 24 años y su mamá, Ermengard von Lothringen, tenía 26 años. Se casó con Regelindis de Lorraine en 1025. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 1 hija. Murió aproximadamente 1063, en Namur, Heiliges Römisches Reich, a la edad de 67 años.
